definition: to get or obtain something
example: The company plans to acquire a smaller competitor next year.

definition: a list of items to be discussed at a meeting
example: Please review the agenda before the board meeting.

definition: to distribute resources for a particular purpose
example: We need to allocate more budget to the marketing department.

definition: a person who applies for a job or position
example: Each applicant must submit a resume and cover letter.

definition: an arrangement to meet someone at a particular time
example: I would like to schedule an appointment with the manager.

definition: to put parts together to make something
example: Workers assemble the products on the factory line.

definition: an official inspection of financial records
example: The annual audit revealed several accounting errors.

definition: producing good results or advantages
example: Regular exercise is beneficial to your health.

definition: an offer to do work or supply goods for a stated price
example: Our company submitted the lowest bid for the project.

definition: a small booklet advertising products or services
example: The travel agency handed out brochures about the resort.

definition: a person being considered for a job or position
example: The committee interviewed five candidates for the role.

definition: a person you work with
example: My colleagues organized a farewell party for the retiring director.

definition: to travel regularly between home and work
example: She commutes two hours every day to reach the office.

definition: to pay someone for work or to make up for a loss
example: The airline will compensate passengers for the delayed flight.

definition: to state that something is true or definite
example: Please confirm your reservation by replying to this email.

definition: following one after another without interruption
example: Sales increased for the third consecutive quarter.

definition: the latest time by which something must be finished
example: We must meet the deadline for the report submission.

definition: to give a task or responsibility to another person
example: A good manager knows how to delegate tasks effectively.

definition: to show how something works or is done
example: The salesperson demonstrated the new software to clients.

definition: able to be trusted and relied upon
example: We need a dependable supplier for our raw materials.

definition: to give or deliver to several people or places
example: The company distributes its products across Asia.

definition: working well without wasting time or resources
example: The new system makes the process much more efficient.

definition: having the right to do or receive something
example: Employees are eligible for the bonus after one year.

definition: to include something with a letter or package
example: Please find the invoice enclosed with this letter.

definition: an approximate calculation of cost or quantity
example: The contractor provided an estimate for the renovation.

definition: money spent on something
example: Travel expenses will be reimbursed by the company.

definition: a building or place used for a particular purpose
example: The factory has state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities.

definition: a prediction of future events or conditions
example: The sales forecast for next quarter looks promising.

definition: to put a plan or decision into effect
example: The company will implement a new policy next month.

definition: the goods and materials a business holds in stock
example: We conduct an inventory check at the end of each month.

definition: a document requesting payment for goods or services
example: The supplier sent an invoice for the delivered goods.

definition: a planned route or schedule for a journey
example: The assistant prepared a detailed itinerary for the business trip.

definition: goods that are bought and sold
example: The store offers a wide range of merchandise.

definition: to discuss in order to reach an agreement
example: Both parties negotiated the terms of the contract.

definition: to get or acquire something
example: You must obtain approval before making the purchase.

definition: a person's job or profession
example: Please state your occupation on the application form.

definition: excellent; or not yet paid or resolved
example: She received an award for her outstanding performance.

definition: time worked beyond normal working hours
example: Employees are paid extra for overtime work.

definition: to delay an event to a later time
example: The meeting was postponed until next Friday.

definition: an established way of doing something
example: Follow the safety procedure when operating the machine.

definition: the rate at which work is completed
example: The new software boosted office productivity.

definition: to advance someone in rank, or to advertise
example: She was promoted to senior manager last year.

definition: to buy something
example: The company purchased new equipment for the lab.

definition: to pay back money that someone has spent
example: The firm will reimburse you for all business expenses.

definition: to repair and improve a building
example: The hotel was renovated to attract more guests.

definition: an arrangement to keep something for someone
example: I made a reservation for four at the restaurant.

definition: the income generated by a business
example: The company's annual revenue exceeded one billion dollars.

definition: a plan listing times of activities
example: The project is ahead of schedule.

definition: a quantity of goods sent together
example: The shipment will arrive at the warehouse on Monday.

definition: a company controlled by another company
example: The corporation owns several subsidiaries overseas.

definition: enough for a particular purpose
example: We have sufficient funds to complete the project.

definition: not certain or fixed; provisional
example: We set a tentative date for the conference.

definition: empty or not occupied
example: There is a vacant position in the sales team.

definition: to check that something is true or accurate
example: Please verify the figures before submitting the report.

definition: a large building for storing goods
example: The products are stored in a warehouse near the port.

definition: a written guarantee for a product
example: The laptop comes with a two-year warranty.
